- 5+ years of technical product or program management experience
- Demonstrated experience owning and delivering large-scale, ambiguous programs with significant business impact

jobs.description:
Project Kuiper is Amazon’s low Earth orbit satellite broadband network. Its mission is to deliver fast, reliable internet to customers and communities around the world, and we’ve designed the system with the capacity, flexibility, and performance to serve a wide range of customers, from individual households to schools, hospitals, businesses, government agencies, and other organizations operating in locations without reliable connectivity.

Export Control Requirement:
Due to applicable export control laws and regulations, candidates must be a U.S. citizen or national, U.S. permanent resident (i.e., current Green Card holder), or lawfully admitted into the U.S. as a refugee or granted asylum.

If you want to tackle complex multi-dimensional problems in countries where Amazon has little to no presence and deliver a solution autonomously, then continue reading.

The Ground Infra Service team defines and manage the delivery of Leo ground infrastructure globally. We facilitate the collaboration and communication across the owners to build ground capacity through Ground Gateway Terminals (GGT) and the necessary infrastructures for when the business needs them. For the service launch and planned service expansions this translates to 450+ sites across 60+ countries needed by 2030. We work backward from the need by date dictated by service launch/expansion to develop unique project plans. We work with teams across a dozen orgs including Legal, Regulations, BizOps, Finance, Network engineering, Ground systems, Entities, Global trade, Operations, Supply chain, Government services, IT and Digital services, Licensing, and Security.
Not only we deliver these sites but we also own their financial health and ensure we stay on budget and support country level P&Ls profitability.

This role handles high-priority, ambiguous initiatives that don’t neatly fit into existing team ownership. You will be expected to define problems, drive cross-functional alignment, and execute with limited structure.

Key job responsibilities

Lead infrastructure strategy and delivery for a geographic region
Own and define ambiguous problem spaces related to global ground infrastructure delivery, establishing clarity where none exists today
Develop and drive multi-year strategy and roadmap for next-generation ground infrastructure deployment globally
Independently lead large-scale, cross-functional initiatives related to new ground infrastructure types, influencing stakeholders across Legal, Regulatory, Finance, Engineering, Operations, and external partners
Design and implement scalable mechanisms and tooling to optimize infrastructure delivery, including capacity planning, financial modeling, latency considerations, and predictive analytics
Identify systemic inefficiencies and lead organization-wide improvements that reduce cost, accelerate timelines, and improve decision quality across regions


A day in the life
Work cross functionality with business teams, finance, operations, and software/network teams
Strategize with regional teams on gateways delivery
Break up roadblocks for your teammates
Track finances and improve cost efficiency yoy
Continuously identify tactical and strategic improvement opportunities in our space and drive them to resolution to scale effectively
Communicate the progress and challenges within the working team and during VP level MBRs
Thrive in ambiguity by seeing around corners and pointing out blind spots.
Travel to regions as needed, up to 25% of the time



About the team
We are the glue that brings all components of Leo ground infrastructure together. We harmonize discord through constructive dialogue and focus on consistent delivery through standard processes. We operate with autonomy and prioritize life balance to ensure we always operate at our highest level.
We acknowledge the ambiguity of our space and welcome taking calculated risks. We always succeed or learn how to succeed.

jobs.company_name: Amazon Support Services Costa Rica SRL
jobs.description:
The Opportunity
We're seeking a strategic Program Manager to help Amazon Business sellers grow their sales by removing obstacles and improving their experience. You'll drive programs and process improvements that directly impact seller success (aiming for 15-20% year-over-year sales growth while reducing seller friction by 30%+).
The S3T team supports business sellers on Amazon, and as Program Manager, you'll own cross-functional programs across our global seller support operations. You'll think strategically, make data-driven decisions, and generate significant impact on both seller experience and business outcomes.

What You'll Do
This role offers a unique opportunity to shape how S3T approaches seller support and issue resolution. You'll create more efficient, seller-friendly experiences by driving standardization, AI/automation, and predictability in service delivery.

Key job responsibilities
• Lead Key Programs: Own end-to-end management of critical initiatives including programs that help sellers optimize their product listings, access support tools, and complete business certifications. You'll define roadmaps, set priorities, and ensure on-time delivery.
• Drive Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and integrate AI solutions across internal procedures and external help resources. Design improvements that reduce manual effort and speed up issue resolution.
• Write Strategic Documents: Author critical business documents including program narratives, strategy documents, operational planning materials, and quarterly business reviews that communicate program health and direction to senior leadership.
• Make Data-Driven Decisions: Use seller performance data and operational metrics to identify opportunities, measure impact, and drive improvements. Build dashboards and reporting mechanisms to track program success and promote the creation of new metrics.
• Manage Stakeholders: Partner with Sales teams, Tech Leadership, Product Managers, and operational teams to identify opportunities, align priorities, remove blockers, and ensure successful outcomes.
• Collaborate Cross-Functionally: Work across organizational boundaries to coordinate with Brand Registry, Risk and Compliance, Priority Catalog, and other internal teams. Drive alignment and remove barriers to program success.
• Champion AI Innovation: Identify opportunities to leverage generative AI, machine learning, and automation to transform seller support workflows. Partner with Tech teams to pilot, test, and scale AI solutions that improve seller experience and operational efficiency.

Who You'll Work With
You'll report to the AB3P Costa Rica Leader and partner closely with Sales Operations and various Sales teams including Account Managers, New Seller Recruitment teams, and Global Account Managers. You'll also collaborate with Tech Teams, Product teams, and operational stakeholders to identify opportunities, design solutions, and execute programs that scale.

What Success Looks Like
You'll balance multiple priorities effectively:
• Drive measurable improvements in seller sales growth
• Maintain high seller satisfaction scores
• Minimize operational friction and manual processes
• Deliver continuous improvement through AI/ML solutions that reduce manual processing time.
• Achieve measurable operational excellence

A day in the life
Your work directly enables thousands of sellers to grow their businesses on Amazon.
You might start your morning reviewing program health metrics, identifying unresolved issues and blockers across in-flight initiatives. Throughout the day, you'll align with Sales teams to understand seller pain points, collaborate with Tech Leadership on automation opportunities, and work with operations to optimize processes.
You could spend part of your day in a working session designing an AI-powered solution to reduce manual processing time, then draft a strategic program document for senior leadership review. Your afternoon might involve removing blockers for a critical launch or identifying sales growth opportunities and proposing a new program to leadership.
You'll resolve escalations, track adoption of new features, and course-correct programs to ensure sellers and account teams can execute with confidence. You're constantly asking: How can we promote sales growth through better seller support? How can we automate this process? How do we scale this solution?
Your focus is on driving impact for sellers and building durable solutions that anticipate future needs, not just solving today's problems.

About the team
The Seller Success Services Team (S3T) owns comprehensive support services for Amazon Business sellers across catalog management, package hierarchy, brand support, pricing operations, quality assurance, and issue resolution.
Our primary objective is to drive growth for seller success within the Amazon Business marketplace by resolving issues for strategic sellers, giving Account Representatives time back to focus on selling. We support and collaborate with Sales teams across multiple segments and manage programs that directly impact seller experience and business outcomes.
Program Management within S3T drives Go-To-Market strategy, change management, automation initiatives, and cross-organizational execution so every launch lands cleanly and every process scales efficiently. We turn seller insights and operational pain points into durable system changes, and we communicate that work through rigorous written documents: program narratives, strategy docs, operational inputs, and regular business reviews.
We measure success when friction falls, resolution times decrease, and sellers grow faster with fewer interruptions.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 5+ years of non-internship professional software development experience
- 5+ years of programming with at least one software programming language experience
- 5+ years of leading design or architecture (design patterns, reliability and scaling) of new and existing systems experience
- Experience as a mentor, tech lead or leading an engineering team
jobs.description:
Ever wonder what happens when S3 processes a PUT request, when EC2 launches a new instance, when DynamoDB commits a write with single-digit millisecond latency, or when Kinesis Data Streams ingests a new record? Behind the scenes, a distributed log service is making it all possible — and you'd be the one shaping its future.

The Transactional Services organization builds foundational distributed systems that power the largest AWS services. We specialize in high-performance, strongly consistent data systems that operate at scale. Our systems process over 4 million writes per second today and are growing fast. When we ship, the blast radius is AWS itself.

We have a multi-year roadmap packed with hard, unsolved problems: building next-generation data export services, driving large-scale migrations that will reshape Amazon's storage infrastructure, and pushing the boundaries of durability, availability, and performance in distributed log systems. This isn't incremental work — it's foundational.

As a Sr. SDE, you'll be a technical leader on a team of 12 engineers. You'll set the technical direction for critical services, drive cross-team architecture decisions, and own the delivery of our most complex, ambiguous projects. You'll partner closely with Principal Engineers and your engineering manager to define the roadmap, and you'll mentor SDE-I and SDE-II engineers to raise the bar across the team.

If you want to lead the design and evolution of distributed systems at AWS scale — not just contribute to them — this is the role.

Key job responsibilities
- Lead the design and architecture of highly available, low-latency distributed log and database systems that process millions of writes per second for AWS's most critical services.
- Own the team's most complex and ambiguous technical problems — define the approach, drive consensus, and deliver end-to-end from problem statement through production.
- Set technical direction across multiple foundational services, making architecture decisions that balance performance, durability, and operational simplicity.
- Lead multi-year, cross-team efforts with high-profile partner teams such as S3 — driving alignment, managing dependencies, and delivering results across organizational boundaries.
- Mentor and develop SDE-I and SDE-II engineers through design reviews, code reviews, and hands-on pairing — building the team's next generation of senior engineers.
- Raise the engineering bar by defining best practices, driving operational excellence, and shaping the team's technical culture.

About the team
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

jobs.description:
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is seeking an experienced Executive Assistant to support two executives in the Data Center Planning and Delivery organization. The successful candidate will work closely with these executives, their teams, and other administrative professionals across the globe.

This exciting role requires superior attention to detail, ability to meet tight deadlines, great organizational skills, and ability to juggle multiple critical requests simultaneously. A high level of integrity and discretion in handling confidential information, and professionalism in dealing with leaders both at and outside Amazon is important, as is the ability to be flexible and change direction seamlessly. The successful candidate will possess the ability to complete complex tasks and projects quickly with little guidance, react with appropriate urgency to situations that require a quick turnaround, and want to take on varied and exciting new projects and learn new skills. You’ll need to take effective action without knowing the total picture, and efficiently and proactively solve difficult or complex problems that affect people within the department or other related groups.

Responsibilities for this position include the ability to build strategic proactive business partnerships, be a best practice leader in the organization, to think and plan ahead, delegate responsibilities appropriately, and manage time effectively. You must work effectively within a team environment, build positive working relationships, maintain a comprehensive awareness of the organization, and become the “go-to” person for support activities both locally and globally.

Key job responsibilities
- Manage complex calendars and scheduling requirements across global time zones, while maintaining diplomacy and discretion.
- Proactively coordinate and engage with leadership in planning and organizing events, activities, and calendars.
- Organize, prioritize, and appropriately handle time-sensitive, confidential information and ensure action required is addressed in a timely manner.
- Organize, execute, and assist with team activities such as staff meeting agendas, on-sites/off-sites, team social events, supply ordering, and cyclical events such as annual planning sessions and performance reviews.
- Arrange travel for executives, both domestic and international; create detailed travel itineraries, including smooth transitions between time zones; coordinate with in-house travel agents and manage international travel processes, including visas.
- Manage and submit expense reports in a timely fashion.
- Own large event planning, including managing group logistics, space planning, and coordinating venues, catering, etc.
- Act as a liaison for your executive’s direct reports and be the single threaded owner of team events and meetings.
- Understand business priorities and identify process improvements to create efficiency and scale for the business.
- Have fun at work and help others to do the same.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating - that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.description:
Join Amazon Web Services (AWS) and lead the optimization of one of the world's largest cloud fleets. We are seeking a software development manager for the Elastic Block Store (EBS) Capacity Engineering team to lead a group of engineers focused on optimizing the fleet of servers powering some of the world's largest websites and cloud services. EBS is the solid state drive for the Internet, storing exabytes of data for millions of customers. You will lead the development and operations of software services that predict future demand and drive orders for additional servers in each AWS data center in the world. We continue to optimize capacity to improve the availability of EBS for all customers and push boundaries of cost efficiencies at massive scale, and we want you to own that charter!

In this role, you will lead, coach, and grow a team of engineers responsible for creating and optimizing global and regional capacity. The team you will lead is in the process of inventing a new capacity forecasting and planning platform for EBS. This involves building software and mathematical optimization models to balance service, infrastructure, financial, and business constraints. EBS is rapidly expanding to meet the growing data usage by AWS customers, accelerated by the exponential growth of AI workloads. You will lead your team to introduce new machine learning models, algorithms, and agents to automate how we plan and purchase capacity to meet this demand growth. As a result, your work will directly impact AWS’s expansion. You will have the opportunity to work across AWS organizations and business teams to power the rapid growth of the AWS cloud and our customers’ products. Your role will ensure optimal operation of our fleet, impacting exabytes of storage and gigawatts of power.

Managing capacity in data centers to avoid outages while ensuring efficient resource use is challenging, and Amazon has developed sophisticated models to anticipate how much and when capacity is needed. You will lead your team to improve these models, which has a direct impact on EBS margins and AWS capital and power efficiency.

Key job responsibilities
Build and maintain a high-performing, world-class team, including hiring the best talent and owning team members' career development and well-being to ensure continued growth in capability over time

Maximize the team's success by ensuring clarity in the team's vision, priorities, roadmap. Use, track, and develop key performance metrics to track progress. Balanced near-term operational needs with high-impact, long-term initiatives.

Write clear, concise documents to communicate vision, proposals, plans, and status updates to stakeholders, including senior leaders across product, engineering, and finance. Develop and analyze business and technical data, as well as what-if scenarios, to drive high-level executive decision-making around infrastructure and product.

Steer technical decisions by guiding your team to make the right technical investments and raise the bar on technical designs. Partner closely with senior and principal engineers to ensure the right technical decisions are made. Invent new solutions to optimize capacity planning using AI/ML.

Lead daily team meetings and process, including creating a high-performing agile environment for the team. Ensure the software development engineers have clarity on their daily work and are unblocked to deliver results for customers.

Organize operations and manage escalations with high velocity to root cause and mitigate impact related to capacity constraints in a site. Identify capacity-related issues proactively and work across technical and business teams to define and implement solutions.

Develop and maintain strong relationships with cross-functional partners across technical and business functions. Participate in leadership discussions across organizations on topics related to capacity management.

Partner with leadership to influence and drive org design and broader prioritization.

A day in the life
Your day will involve leading daily agile ceremonies, guiding technical architecture decisions, meeting with customers, and interfacing with stakeholders and senior leaders to review capacity plans for key EBS feature launches. You'll be a facilitator and consensus builder, helping the team make high-quality, well-informed decisions. This will include authoring, editing, or reviewing technical, product, and business documents (6-page narratives, PRFAQs, 1 pagers). You'll own the optimization of our software systems to enable seamless, cost-effective scaling to meet growing customer demand on AWS. This is a highly visible role in the organization, and requires a strong ownership mindset.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

Mentorship and Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

- High school or equivalent diploma
- Current valid Basic Life Support (BLS) certification by an approved emergency cardiac care (ECC) provider; AND must have one of the following:
- Current valid Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) OR Paramedic Certification from the Department of Health OR the National Registry of Emergency Medical Technicians (NREMT); OR
- Current Active Athletic Trainer Certification by either the Board of Certification (BOC) or equivalent State Certification
jobs.description:
Join Amazon’s mission to become Earth’s safest place to work! At Amazon, we’ve set the ambitious goal to become the benchmark of safety excellence across all industries in which we operate. The Amazon Workplace Health & Safety (WHS) team sets the strategic direction to provide resources, best practices, and safe environments to proactively manage the health and wellbeing of our workforce. To support these goals, Amazon is seeking an experienced and dynamic Onsite Medical Representative (OMR) to join our team. In this position, you will work closely with the WHS Site Manager, WHS Specialist, and occasionally the Injury Prevention Specialist (IPS). This includes attending weekly associate safety committee meetings, greeting all new hires for the site or through daily one-on-one interaction. Additionally, the OMR will participate in weekly case management review meetings as necessary. As an OMR, you will independently assess and administer first aid as well as offer guidance to Amazon Associates (AAs) on both occupational and non-occupational injuries or illnesses. You should be passionate about learning and advocating health and wellness principles to consistently support AAs on a daily basis. You will refer care to outside medical providers as needed, and learn about OSHA regulations, risk, and analysis. The OMR will function as a part of the larger team onsite, assisting with all first aid needs and support safety operations as needed.

Responsibilities include but are not limited to:
- Provide first aid support and make referrals to outside medical providers as necessary.
- Minimize the AA risk of injury through awareness, education, and proactive engagement.
- Maintain all records of care provided to AAs.
- Coordinate and maintain the workers’ compensation and return to work programs for AAs with work-related injuries.
- Provide daily activity logs and end of shift reports.
- Engage with AAs and leadership on operations floor to provide coaching regarding observed at-risk work habits.
- Maintain a clean working environment and ensure appropriate medical supply inventory is maintained.
- Participate in training and certification to facilitate first aid, CPR and AED certifications classes.
- Maintain all first aid, CPR, AED credentials.
- Work flexible shifts which could include days, nights, holidays, and/or weekends.
- Assist with random saliva drug testing protocols.
- Maintain effective care delivery in emergencies and assist emergency response at the site.

Additional Job Elements include:
- Lift up to 49 pounds and frequently push, pull, squat, bend, and reach
- Stand/walk for up to 12 hours during shifts
- Work in an environment where the noise level varies
- Work in an environment that is subject to variable temperatures and weather (delivery stations include outside loading departments)
- Continuously climb and descend stairs (applies to sites with stairs)

For those that do not have an adult First Aid, CPR, AED certification from the American Heart Association or American Red Cross, must obtain within 3 weeks of start date.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- PhD, or Master's degree and 4+ years of quantitative field research experience
- Experience investigating the feasibility of applying scientific principles and concepts to business problems and products
- Experience analyzing both experimental and observational data sets
jobs.description:
The Alexa 1P Devices and Marketing organization is looking for an experienced Research Scientist who is energized by the opportunity to help build the future of AI through Alexa+. You will be joining the Alexa Customer Science (ACS) team, whose mission is to surface timely, scientifically-grounded customer insights that help ground Alexa product and marketing decisions.

The ideal candidate will have a strong background in quantitative research methods, excellent analytical skills, comfort with ambiguous problem spaces and large-n quantitative and qualitative data sets, and a passion for understanding individual-level attitudes and behavior. Experience with sampling design and survey data weighting is desirable. General AI fluency is a requirement for this role, and experience with AI research workflow integrations is highly desirable.

As a Research Scientist with ACS, you will be responsible for designing, conducting, and analyzing research that helps product and marketing teams in Alexa Devices better understand our customers' preferences, experiences, and behaviors. You will own research projects end-to-end, including measurement strategy, data collection, analysis, and reporting to peers and business leaders. You will work closely with cross-functional teams, including product managers, marketing managers, UX researchers and data scientists, designers, and engineers. You should have deep expertise in the design, creation, management, and business use of surveys, randomized experiments (including conjoints, maxdiff, and other choice tasks), and the latest research tools, including those leveraging AI applications. Competency in mixed-methods or qualitative approaches is also desirable.

Key job responsibilities
- Work closely with product and marketing teams, as well as fellow researchers, to identify research topics and build a research roadmap; communicate and refresh on a regular basis to ensure relevancy.

- Create a deep understanding of customers through descriptive, inferential, and experimental approaches (existing or invented) that you identify as being most effective for answering a given business question.
- Design, implement, and analyze data from surveys, randomized experiments, and other large-n data sets.
- Work with data engineering and business intelligence teams to triangulate survey data with customer engagement and segmentation data.
- Create repeatable and scalable mechanisms to measure key customer metrics that drive product iteration.
- Synthesize a wide range of primary and secondary data types leading to focused, insightful, and actionable insights that persuade and inspire partners and leaders to take concerted, informed actions.
- Work closely with research peers to promote best practices, build resources, and train team members to enable them to execute their own research projects.

A day in the life
Here at Amazon, we embrace our differences. We are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. Amazon has ten employee-led affinity groups, reaching 40,000 employees in over 190 chapters globally. We have innovative benefit offerings, and host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 16 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious and earn trust.

About the team
The Alexa Customer Science (ACS) team is a team of Research Scientists, UX Researchers, and Market Researchers that surfaces timely, scientifically-grounded insights about customers to guide marketing and product leaders.

jobs.description:
At Amazon Worldwide Grocery Stores through Whole Foods Market, we're more than a grocery store — we’re a mission-driven community focused on nourishing people and the planet. As our Sr. Category Merchant Manager, you’ll play a key role in shaping the future of food by turning supplier relationships into meaningful, long-term partnerships that are rooted in shared values and mutual growth.
In this high-impact role, you'll lead strategic negotiations with our most important suppliers, distributors, and third-party vendors moving beyond transactions to cultivate transformational alliances. From joint business planning and purpose-aligned top-to-top sessions to data-driven cost negotiations and innovative promotional strategies, you’ll ensure every conversation advances value for our customers, our partners, and our planet.

This position is located in Austin, TX with the expectation of being onsite 5 days a week.

Key job responsibilities
Strategic Partner Management:
• Conducting annual supplier tiering exercise to identify and develop strategic partnerships
• Creating and executing annual partner engagement roadmaps with clear ROI metrics
• Building and maintaining strong relationships with key stakeholders across the organization
• Developing and implementing strategic initiatives to drive profitability and business growth

Financial Management:
• Analyzing and optimizing supplier funding collections to maximize profitability
• Managing P&L impacts of partnership initiatives
• Tracking and reporting financial value creation from partnerships
• Identifying cost-saving opportunities through strategic negotiations

Partnership Development:
• Leading and facilitating key partnership processes including:
o Joint Business Planning
o Top to Top meetings
o Innovation meetings
o Strategic negotiations
• Creating and executing annual supplier communication strategies
• Developing best practices for collaborative engagement
• Coaching category merchants on partnership and negotiation strategies

Skills:

Collaborative Negotiation and Partnering:
• Strong understanding of negotiation frameworks and collaborative approaches
• Ability to identify mutual gains and create value-driven solutions
• Excellence in strategic questioning and active listening
• Effective communication and messaging skills
• Understanding of leverage points and their appropriate use

Business and Financial Acumen:
• Deep understanding of P&L management and financial metrics
• Ability to identify and execute profit-driving initiatives
• Strong analytical skills to evaluate business opportunities
• Understanding of supplier and distributor business models
• Capability to align partnership strategies with financial goals

Facilitation and Leadership:
• Design and lead effective cross-functional meetings and processes
• Drive collaborative problem-solving and innovation
• Ensure accountability and follow-through on commitments
• Build consensus and maintain productive relationships
• Document and track outcomes and action items

Strategic Problem-Solving:
• Identify and overcome business challenges
• Develop solutions that maximize value for Merchandising
• Balance competing priorities and interests
• Drive profitable growth through strategic thinking

jobs.description:
Work hard, have fun, and make history with Amazon’s International Public Policy team. The Head of Public Policy, KSA will will join a growing and dynamic team that works across Europe, Middle East, Africa, India and Asia Pacific, in support of Amazon’s global consumer and digital businesses. Like our business interests, our policy issues are broad, forward-thinking, and driven by what is best for our customers. The International Public Policy team works with a wide range of national, local, and supranational policymakers, industry organizations, and non-governmental organizations across numerous policy issues spanning the consumer and commercial environment.

Job responsibilities

As Head of Public Policy, KSA you will be responsible for working with policymakers and non-governmental groups in support of our consumer-centric business model within the country. You will have an astute understanding of the public policy and regulatory environment as well as the key stakeholders within it. You will coordinate across Amazon’s business, locally and globally, to develop and implement positioning and engagement strategies.

You will help design external advocacy efforts, outreach programs, partnerships, and initiatives in support of these business objectives. You will help coordinate Amazon’s engagement with relevant industry associations and wider coalitions, and manage consultancy support. You will be able to build solutions for solutions for complicated business problems, and provide clear and confident guidance, including in situations of high ambiguity. You will be confident, but are also able to operate respectfully and collaboratively within wider regional and international structures.

- Project management experience.
- Outstanding oral, written, and interpersonal communication skills.
- A self-starter who can take general direction and produce specific results.
- Able to coordinate a wide array of internal and external resources to obtain, process, and operationalize information and strategy.
- Strong judgment and decision-making skills.
- Capable of confidently using experience and data to assess and recommend a best course of action even in an ambiguous situation.

jobs.description:
The Amazon.ca Fashion team is seeking a self-driven Vendor Manager candidate to support our Vendor Management team and our vendor partners. Vendor Managers work directly with vendors to improve value, selection and convenience across their business. They play a critical role in managing strategic and established brands to drive profitability and top line performance for their category.
The candidate will use customer obsession and analytics to drive their business. The Vendor Manager will have the opportunity to engage with multiple internal teams to optimize product line performance for key manufacturers (vendors) on Amazon.ca. They will utilize strong communication skills to collaborate with internal cross-functional partners, such as site merchandising, buying, pricing, inventory management, operations, online marketing, and finance to drive performance for our Amazon.ca vendor partners.

This position provides the candidate an opportunity to execute analytical deeps dives that help inform category growth strategies and initiatives. In addition to gaining vendor management experience, this candidate will have exposure in driving category growth. Amazon.ca Vendor Managers get the unique opportunity to work at the most innovative online retailer, while developing strong functional skills in online merchandising/marketing, buying, planning/forecasting, pricing, and business analytics.

Vendor Manager roles are in the General Management career path at Amazon. This role is an excellent introduction to a wide variety of business functions and provides advancement opportunities to more senior roles in Marketing/Merchandising, Vendor Management, Product Management and Inventory Management.

Key job responsibilities
Key job responsibilities
- P&L Management
- Vendor Negotiations
- New Product Launches
- Ownership of key team-level business functions (pricing, selection, profitability)
- Defining vendor and category level growth strategies
